lesson.title: Know Your Constitutional Rights
lesson.explanation: The SA Constitution (1996) is the supreme law. Chapter 2 Bill of Rights guarantees: Section 9 (equality), Section 10 (human dignity), Section 12 (freedom and security), Section 14 (privacy), Section 16 (freedom of expression), Section 23 (fair labour practices), Section 24 (clean environment), Section 25 (property), Section 26 (housing), Section 27 (healthcare, food, water, social security), Section 28 (children's rights), Section 29 (education). These rights apply to everyone in SA. Limitations only allowed per Section 36 (reasonable and justifiable in an open and democratic society).

examples.problem: Practical scenario in Know Your Constitutional Rights

practice_problems.question: What is the first step when beginning work in Know Your Constitutional Rights?
Describe the key regulation or standard that governs Know Your Constitutional Rights in South Africa.
practice_problems.answer: See lesson content for specific SA regulations.
hints: Think about SANS standards or Acts
practice_problems.explanation: SA has specific standards for each trade.

key_vocabulary.definition: Key concept in Know Your Constitutional Rights

misconceptions.misconception: You don't need formal training for Know Your Constitutional Rights
misconceptions.correction: Proper training and certification are required for safety and legal compliance in SA.
misconceptions.why_students_think_this: People see informal work and assume no qualifications are needed.
